license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.vh5e386
title: Air volume demonstration for children
author: Unknown

steps_raw.instruction: Take a large bowl and fill with water, almost to the top.
Get a dry cup that can fully fit inside the bowl.
Add tape to the bottom of the cup (double-sided or rolled up to be sticky from both sides).
Crumple a piece of napkin or paper towel.
Insert the paper towel into the cup, attaching to the bottom. The paper should be fully inside the cup, away from the open edge.
Carefully push the cup, face down, into the bowl. Do not tilt the cup.
Submerge the cup fully, so the water covers the top of the cup.
Carefully lift the cup, removing it from the bowl without tilting. Once removed, tilt slightly and let the water drip off.
With your dry hand, remove the paper towel from the cup.
